articles.title: Getty Images calls off Shutterstock merger after UK regulator's divestment demand
articles.summary: Getty Image has ended its planned $3.7 billion merger with Shutterstock. The UK competition regulator required Shutterstock to sell its editorial business. Getty's board decided this condition was not in the company's best interests.

articles.title: Why France is finally accepting air conditioners after years of saying 'no'
articles.summary: Only around 24% of French households have air conditioning, according to France's energy transition agency.

articles.title: India strengthens position as investment destination; FDI rises by 44% to $39 billion in 2025
articles.summary: South Asia witnessed a sharp rise in FDI inflows, which climbed from $34 billion to $46 billion, largely driven by India, where inflows expanded to $39 billion, the report noted.

articles.title: Wall Street sees mixed performance; Dow rises 0.3%, Nasdaq falls 0.8% as AI stocks slide
articles.summary: US stocks traded mixed on Tuesday as technology shares faced pressure. Declines in artificial intelligence and semiconductor stocks weighed on the Nasdaq Composite. The S&P 500 also slipped, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average saw gains.

articles.title: Mahadev case: ED seeks permanent attachment of Rs 940.77 crore assets linked to Vikas Garg
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate has moved the Adjudicating Authority seeking confirmation of the provisional attachment of properties worth Rs 940.77 crore linked to EBIX Inc Chairman Vikas Garg in the Mahadev Online Betting syndicate money laundering case.

articles.title: India urges US to reconsider proposed 12.5% tariff over 'forced labour' probe under USTR's Sec 301
articles.summary: India has asked the United States to withdraw proposed tariffs on its imports. The nation argues the US lacks adequate evidence for its claims. India's submission highlights a broad approach by the USTR investigation.

articles.title: El Nino impact: India’s hydropower generation sees steepest drop since Feb 2024; piles pressure on grid
articles.summary: Data from the India Meteorological Department showed that cumulative rainfall across the country was 38% below normal until July 1, largely due to the development of El Niño conditions over the Pacific Ocean, which affected the southwest monsoon running from June to September.

articles.title: SEBI issues recovery notice to former DHFL promoter Dheeraj Wadhawan over ₹28.62 crore penalty
articles.summary: According to the recovery certificate dated July 7, 2026, SEBI has directed Wadhawan to pay ₹28.62 crore within 15 days of receipt of the notice.

articles.title: India bets on critical minerals, rare earths and chips partnerships
articles.summary: India has launched the National Critical Mineral Mission (NCMM) in January 2025 for a seven-year period from 2024-25 to 2030-31. The mission goes far beyond a conventional mining initiative.

articles.title: Rajesh Exports case: NFRA begins probe after Sebi flags alleged ₹15.15 lakh crore mismatch
articles.summary: The National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) has begun its probe into Rajesh Exports, nearly a month after the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) alleged that the company misrepresented about ₹15.15 lakh crore in revenues in its interim order.

articles.title: 'Instead of selling 10-acre plots...': Indian entrepreneur urges Centre to create ready-to-use factory parks for first-time founders
articles.summary: The entrepreneur proposed ready-to-use factory units equipped with electricity, regulatory approvals, shared machinery and logistics facilities, allowing first-time founders to test manufacturing ideas without buying land.

articles.title: Paris Couture Week 2026: Isha Ambani, Cardi B front row as Rahul Mishra's 'Devi' reimagines India's ancient temples for the modern goddess
articles.summary: Inspired by the divine feminine, the collection transformed the artistic language of the Ajanta and Ellora caves, a 12th-century stone dancer from Karnataka, murals and architecture of the Tarakeshwara Temple into clothes that felt less like garments and more …

articles.title: India's economy passed the Iran war test. Could El Nino spoil the party?
articles.summary: Economists say that the world’s fastest growing economy may even end up clocking a 7% GDP growth number this year. But, domestically a poor monsoon caused by El Nino is painting a grim picture for inflation. Is the worst really over for the Indian economy?

articles.title: China weighs curbs on overseas access to their advanced AI models: Report
articles.summary: Chinese officials are discussing restricting overseas access to advanced artificial intelligence models. These talks involve major technology firms like Alibaba and ByteDance. The government views cutting-edge AI as a strategic national asset amid global competition.

articles.title: Did you know Vande Bharat has aircraft-style toilets and panoramic windows? Here's what else
articles.summary: Vande Bharat has automatic sliding doors, bio-vacuum toilets, panoramic windows and per-seat charging sockets. Here are 5 hidden features most passengers don't notice onboard.

articles.title: Tata Punch is India's best-selling car of June 2026 — here's every variant and feature explained
articles.summary: Tata Punch topped India's June 2026 sales chart with 21,006 units. Available in petrol, CNG and EV, here's a complete look at specs, features, BaaS pricing and charging details.

articles.title: The biggest hurdle in India's chip race isn't the plan—it's execution
articles.summary: India's semiconductor strategy draws on proven Asian models for growth. Execution, not strategy, presents the nation's primary challenge now. Developing skilled workers and strengthening local supply chains are crucial steps.

articles.title: 10 life-changing lessons from Walter Isaacson's Elon Musk biography you need to read
articles.summary: Walter Isaacson's biography of Elon Musk is packed with life lessons on obsession, risk, failure and leadership. Here are the 10 most powerful takeaways from the acclaimed book.

articles.title: 5 best entry-level luxury cars in India 2026: Mini, Audi, Volvo, Mercedes and BMW compared
articles.summary: From Volvo EX30 at ₹41 lakh to Mercedes CLA Electric at ₹55 lakh, here are 5 entry-level luxury cars worth considering as your first premium car purchase in India in 2026.

articles.title: Flights, hotels, visa in one go: 10 things to know about Saudi’s Package Visa
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia has introduced a new pilot Package Visa service for tourists. This digital initiative integrates flight and accommodation bookings with visa applications. Eligible travelers can now book their entire trip through a single, unified travel package.

articles.title: Earn Abroad : These 10 Countries That Literally Pay You to Move
articles.summary: Discover 10 countries that pay you to move, offering grants, free housing, tax breaks, and work visas. From Chile to Italy, these incentives create unique opportunities for entrepreneurs and families.

articles.title: Billionaire Hotspots: Where The Ultra-Rich Are Spending Millions
articles.summary: Discover the world’s top luxury cities where billionaires spend millions. Singapore, Zurich, Monaco, Hong Kong, and London lead in elite real estate, cars, lifestyle, and exclusivity.

articles.title: Can the new carbon credit scheme clean up India’s steel sector?
articles.summary: India's new emissions trading scheme aims to drive greener production, but long-term investments will determine its success.

articles.title: Forget Business School: Ankur Warikoo Learned Life’s Sharpest Lessons from Movies
articles.summary: Ankur Warikoo reveals 7 films that taught him career-defining lessons in resilience, trust, ambition, strategy, execution, and lifelong learning beyond the MBA classroom.

articles.title: Unseen Impact — Small Gestures That Define Big Management Success
articles.summary: Small gestures define great managers. From personalized feedback to first-week impact, these subtle actions shape team culture, trust, and long-term organizational success.

articles.title: Amitabh Bachchan called at 3 AM to buy land in Ayodhya; transferred Rs 15 cr in no time: Abhinandan Lodha
articles.summary: He further said the request stood out because such large plots are rarely sought by individual buyers.

articles.summary: Discover 9 smartphones with 200MP cameras delivering unmatched detail, pro-level photography, AI enhancements, long-lasting batteries, and immersive displays for creators and gamers.

articles.title: IIT Bombay gold medallist said no to ₹2.9 crore US job for his parents — here’s the full story
articles.summary: IIT Bombay gold medallist Vivek Sharma turned down a ₹2.9 crore US job to care for his ailing parents. Years later, the same company returned with a remote offer. His story went viral.

articles.title: Tata Motors July 2026 EV discounts: Save up To ₹3.35 lakh on Curvv, Harrier, Punch and more
articles.summary: Tata Motors is offering discounts of up to ₹3.35 lakh on its EVs in July 2026. From Curvv EV to Nexon EV, here's every model, every offer and exactly how much you can save.

articles.title: 'Dumbest thing I've ever done': Man who quit $200,000 job at Microsoft to become content creator
articles.summary: According to a viral video on Instagram, Christian Harms left his high-paying role at Microsoft at the age of 26 and moved to Australia with the dream of building a business and creating content

articles.title: Back with a bang! Foreign investors bet on financials, record fortnightly inflows seen in second half of June
articles.summary: The renewed interest in financial stocks helped overseas investors emerge as net buyers of Indian equities worth more than Rs 14,000 crore during the June 16-30 period, reversing net outflows of Rs 63,450 crore recorded in the first fortnight of the month, according to NSDL data.

articles.title: Want a stake in Cochin Shipyard? Centre launches offer for sale at Rs 1,400 per share
articles.summary: The Centre is selling a 2.52% stake in Cochin Shipyard Limited through an Offer for Sale. Non-retail investors can bid on July 7, 2026, and retail investors on July 8, 2026. This divestment supports the government's extensive maritime ecosystem expansion programs.

articles.title: India's strategic gas storage: LNG buffer mandate for terminals in the works - what it means
articles.summary: The disruption to LNG shipments through the Strait of Hormuz during the Iran conflict highlighted India's exposure to supply disruptions, prompting the government to revisit the idea of creating strategic gas reserves.

articles.title: IIM campus, UPI, AI & more: PM Modi heralds 'golden chapter' of India, Indonesia ties
articles.summary: India and Indonesia are enhancing cooperation in defense and maritime security. Discussions covered trade, energy, and critical minerals for future growth. Both nations agreed to boost technology sharing and startup collaborations.

articles.title: Rupee recovers 15 paise to 95.28 against US dollar in early trade
articles.summary: The Indian rupee opened stronger against the US dollar on Tuesday. Lower crude oil price expectations and easing Middle East tensions improved market sentiment. Saudi Arabia's decision to cut oil prices for Asian buyers added to the positive mood.

articles.title: Kospi's AI party over? South Korean benchmark tumbles 7%, sell-side sidecar activated
articles.summary: South Korean stocks experienced a significant decline on Tuesday. Investors rushed to secure profits after strong earnings reports were released. Heavyweight technology stocks led the broad sell-off across the market.

articles.title: Stock market opens in green as Sensex jumps over 80 points, Nifty50 trades above 24,470
Oil price today: Crude benchmarks inch higher as focus shifts to supply, demand and Hormuz shipments
articles.summary: Oil prices saw a slight increase Tuesday after a tanker was struck near the Strait of Hormuz. This incident raised security concerns on a vital global oil shipping route. However, market gains were limited as traders focused on rising crude supplies.

articles.title: 'Growth Gems' biz to power Mahindra's next leap
articles.summary: Mahindra Group is expanding into fast-growing businesses beyond its core operations. The conglomerate has set ambitious expansion targets across multiple sectors. These "Growth Gems" are positioned to benefit from structural economic trends.
